page: Google Cloud Platform m2-hypermem-416 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by Intel Xeon x86_64 processors. It features 416 dedicated vCPUs derived from 208 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with an extensive memory capacity of 8832.0 GB of RAM, translating to approximately 21.23 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates exceptional multi-core processing and memory subsystem performance. It achieves top-tier results in stress-ng multi-core CPU testing and Passmark CPU suites, including extended instructions, encryption, and physics tests. Memory bandwidth is highly optimized, reaching top-tier speeds for both cached small-block reads and uncached large-block read and write operations. Additionally, the platform excels in high-throughput scenarios, delivering top-tier performance in Redis SET operations and static web serving. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by lower scores in single-threaded stress-ng, Geekbench, and gzip compression tests.

Given its massive memory footprint and high core count, the m2-hypermem-416 represents a specialized hardware class designed for resource-dense workloads. While single-threaded efficiency is limited, the sheer scale of memory and multi-core capabilities makes it highly effective for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, massive data caching layers, and complex multi-threaded simulations.
description: Google Cloud Platform m2-hypermem-416 is a memory-optimized server configured with 416 dedicated vCPUs and 8832.0 GB of RAM. Powered by Intel Xeon x86_64 processors running at 2.7 GHz across 208 physical cores, this instance is designed for massive memory-bound workloads. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high-throughput capabilities in Redis operations and static web serving. Conversely, single-core performance and single-threaded compression tasks are relatively weak. This instance is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise ERP applications, and heavy multi-threaded data processing tasks where massive memory capacity is critical.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m2-hypermem-416 is a memory-optimized server featuring 416 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 8.625 TB of RAM, designed for massive in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m2-hypermem-416 offers 416 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 8.625 TB of RAM, optimized for massive in-memory database workloads.
tagline: A massive memory-optimized instance featuring over eight terabytes of RAM and four hundred dedicated virtual cores for enterprise in-memory workloads.
bullet_points: 416 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 8832.0 GB of RAM
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases and Redis deployments
Weaker single-core and single-threaded processing speeds
